int.description: Signed integer type, 64-bit on modern platforms, for whole number values

string.description: Binary-safe sequence of bytes, used for text with single or double quotes

bool.description: Boolean type with values true and false; many values are falsy including 0, '', [], null

array.description: Ordered map that serves as array, list, hash table, dictionary, and stack

object.description: Instance of a class, created with new or cast from arrays and stdClass

null.description: Special type with only one value null, represents a variable with no value

callable.description: Any value that can be called as a function: closures, function names, [class, method] arrays

void.description: Return type indicating a function returns no value (PHP 7.1+)

mixed.description: Union type accepting any value, equivalent to all types combined (PHP 8.0+)

never.description: Return type for functions that always throw or exit (PHP 8.1+)

iterable.description: Pseudo-type accepting arrays and objects implementing Traversable (PHP 7.1+)
